product_description: Usage-based cloud cost monitoring and optimization platform migrating from flat-rate to consumption-based pricing

tickets.title: Design and implement usage-based billing engine
tickets.description: Migrate from flat monthly pricing ($49/$99/$199 tiers) to consumption-based pricing where customers pay per metric ingested and per dashboard query. Requires a metering pipeline that tracks usage in real time, a rating engine to apply pricing rules, and integration with Stripe for invoicing.

tickets.title: Build pricing calculator and cost estimator for customers
tickets.description: Customers migrating to usage-based pricing need transparency on expected costs. Build an in-app pricing calculator that estimates monthly costs based on current usage patterns, and a predictive cost dashboard showing projected spend for the next billing cycle.

tickets.title: Implement usage alerts and spending caps
tickets.description: To prevent bill shock during pricing transition, customers need configurable spending alerts (50%, 75%, 90%, 100% of budget) and optional hard spending caps that pause non-critical data ingestion when exceeded. Must handle graceful degradation without data loss.

tickets.title: Grandfather existing customers with 12-month price lock
tickets.description: Product and legal agreed to honor current pricing for existing customers for 12 months after the switch. Need a grandfathering system that tracks customer cohorts, applies correct pricing tiers, and automatically transitions customers after the lock period with 60-day advance notice.

tickets.title: Customer churn risk analysis for pricing migration
tickets.description: Finance and product need a model that identifies which customer segments will see cost increases vs. decreases under the new pricing. Flag accounts where projected spend increases >30% for proactive account management outreach. CEOs concern is losing mid-market accounts during transition.

documents.title: competitor_brief.md
documents.content:
# Competitive Brief — CloudMetrics Pricing

## Competitor A: DataDogLite
- **Strengths:** Already consumption-based pricing, transparent cost calculator, generous free tier (50K metrics/month). Strong developer community.
- **Weaknesses:** Costs escalate rapidly at scale (known "bill shock" complaints on social media), limited enterprise support, no custom pricing.
- **Recent moves:** Introduced committed-use discounts (pay upfront for 20% savings) in Q3 2025. Customer satisfaction around pricing is mixed.

## Competitor B: MetricStream
- **Strengths:** Flat-rate pricing that enterprise customers love for budget predictability. All-inclusive tiers with no usage limits.
- **Weaknesses:** Over-provisioned for small customers, no usage-efficient option. Losing startup segment to pay-as-you-go competitors.
- **Recent moves:** Testing a hybrid model in beta: flat base + overage charges above tier limits. Early feedback is negative — perceived as worst of both worlds.

documents.title: customer_notes.md
documents.content:
# Customer Notes — CloudMetrics Pricing Change

## Interview 1: Alex P. (Startup CTO, Current $49/month plan)
Alex ingests fewer than 10,000 metrics per month and runs about 50 dashboard queries daily. Under the new pricing model, his bill would drop to approximately $22/month. "I love the idea of paying for what I use — the flat rate always felt like I was subsidizing bigger customers." However, he wants clear visibility into usage with no surprise charges.

## Interview 2: Linda W. (Director of Platform, Current $199/month plan)
Linda's team ingests 2.5 million metrics per month and runs complex queries across 40 dashboards. Under the new pricing model, her estimated cost would jump to approximately $480/month. "A 140% price increase is going to be a hard conversation with finance. I need at least 6 months notice and a migration path." She wants spending caps and a cost simulator before committing.

## Interview 3: Tom H. (VP Engineering, Enterprise Account, Custom Pricing)
Tom's organization has a custom $15K/month contract negotiated two years ago. His usage has grown 3x since then. "We're actually underpaying relative to usage, so consumption-based pricing might work in our favor long-term, but we need the price lock guarantee during the transition period." He asked for detailed usage reporting and the ability to set per-team budgets within the organization.
